blocks.text: The local runtime now requires page perception outputs to pass quality gates before downstream modules consume high-signal context.
Researchers reported that grounded source references reduce false summaries because every claim keeps a fallback quote and content hash.
The release defines sourceCoverage, groundingCompleteness, jumpbackCoverage, and noiseRatio as required metrics for automated acceptance.
Teams can inspect rejected low-signal blocks in debug evidence without exposing extractor-specific candidate output as final contract data.
The implementation keeps a DOM baseline path so validation can run on local fixtures without network access or optional third-party extractor packages.
Related articles: browser extensions, unrelated product launch, sponsored newsletter
For developers, the key procedure is to validate schema, fixture gates, quality metrics, and boundary checks after every substage.
